title: On Liberty
author: Mill, John Stuart
summary: On Liberty, dedicated to Mrs. Taylor, is John Stuart Mill's essay arguing for individual freedom and the importance of truth, guided by his utilitarian principles and influenced by personal relationships. The provided excerpt includes the publisher's front matter, a moving dedication to Mill's wife, and an introduction sketching Mill's life: his rigorous education, literary career, friendships (Comte, Carlyle), major works, and the powerful influence of Mrs. Taylor on his thought and happiness. The introduction discusses Mill's public service, parliamentary career, and the intellectual partnership with his wife, portraying her as an inspirer whose early death deeply affected him and legacy.

characters.name: John Stuart Mill
characters.personality: intensely intellectual, industrious, reflective, influenced by close personal relationships
characters.lifespan: Born 20 May 1806; career and life sketched in the introduction; died 1873

characters.name: Mrs. Taylor (Mrs. Mill)
characters.personality: vivid, intellectually stimulating, inspirational and influential on Mill
characters.lifespan: Died 1858; buried at Avignon; described as Mill's inspirer and beloved wife

characters.name: James Mill
characters.age: older generation (father of John Stuart Mill)
characters.personality: rigorous educator, disciplinarian intellectual influence
characters.lifespan: Appears as Mill's father and educator in the narrative (no dates given in excerpt)

characters.name: General Sir Samuel Bentham
characters.personality: mentioned as head of family accompanying a youthful Mill visit
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in connection with Mill's visit to the south of France during his youth

characters.personality: friend or acquaintance in Mill's social circle
characters.lifespan: Named among guests at Mr. Taylor's dinner where Mill met Mrs. Taylor

characters.name: W. J. Fox
characters.personality: political/literary acquaintance
characters.lifespan: Present at the dinner where Mill first met Mrs. Taylor

characters.name: Miss Harriet Martineau
characters.personality: writer and acquaintance in Mill's circle
characters.lifespan: Present at the dinner introducing Mill and Mrs. Taylor

characters.name: Mrs. Grote
characters.personality: friend in Mill's circle who objected to Mill's intimacy
characters.lifespan: Mentioned among those who were troubled by Mill's attachment

characters.name: Mrs. Austin
characters.personality: acquaintance affected by Mill's relationship
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as among those who suffered because they alluded to the forbidden subject

characters.name: George Mill
characters.personality: critical observer of Mill's attachment
characters.lifespan: Quoted as giving an opinion about Mrs. Taylor's merits

characters.name: Carlyle (Thomas Carlyle)
characters.personality: distinctive, sometimes critical literary figure; gave epithets to Mrs. Taylor
characters.lifespan: Recounted interaction involving a burned manuscript and subsequent reparations

characters.name: Mrs. Carlyle
characters.personality: expressed judgment that Mrs. Taylor was 'dangerous'
characters.lifespan: Quoted in the introduction concerning Mrs. Taylor

characters.name: Tennyson (Alfred Lord Tennyson)
characters.personality: poet; discovered for his generation by Mill
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as one of Mill's literary influences/discoveries

characters.name: Comte (Auguste Comte)
characters.personality: philosopher and correspondent; intellectual influence in Mill's life
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as a friend and correspondent who influenced Mill for some years

characters.name: Mr. Taylor
characters.personality: host of the dinner where Mill met Mrs. Taylor
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as the owner of the house where the initial meeting took place

characters.name: Mrs. Mill's step-daughter
characters.personality: supportive collaborator (helped on later works)
characters.lifespan: Named as assisting Mill after his wife's death; provided companionship in later years

characters.name: George Grote
characters.personality: historian whose works Mill engaged with
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in relation to Mill's reviews and scholarship

characters.name: W. H. Smith
characters.personality: political opponent
characters.lifespan: Defeated Mill in a subsequent general election as mentioned in the introduction

characters.name: Gladstone (W. E. Gladstone)
characters.personality: statesman who commented on Mill's value to the House of Commons
characters.lifespan: Quoted as praising Mill's contributions to Parliament

characters.name: Dr. Garnett
characters.personality: biographer/commentator (wrote Life of Carlyle)
characters.lifespan: Cited as a source describing Mill's reparations to Carlyle

locations.name: India House (East India House site), London, UK
